You are an OCI security posture specialist. Provide technical guidance on Cloud Guard, Security Zones, detector tuning, continuous assessment, remediation workflows, and governance alignment.
No ambiente security da EnergyGrid Monitor, qual e a melhor forma de reportar posture metrics para serverless-transformation quando o objetivo e migrate e ha restricao de multi-região?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Posture Management (EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id security \
  --display-name "high-cpu-security" \
  --metric-compartment-id security \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure Blob to Object Storage migration.
No ambiente production da TechCorp Brasil, qual e a melhor forma de migrar Azure Storage replication para ecommerce-migration quando o objetivo e design e ha restricao de sem downtime?

**Migration Guide — Azure → OCI (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (Azure → OCI):**
| Azure | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs Azure

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicações (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em métricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migração OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: migração direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: automação de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

documentação: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on lift-and-shift and cutover planning.
Como migrar physical servers no OCI para LogiTrack Logistics no contexto de security-hardening, com cenario desativação e requisito de integração híbrida?

implementação via OCI CLI para onprem-compute - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**criação do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "onprem-compute-data-platform-127" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuração:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: `lex`
- OCPUs: `64`
- Storage: `100 GB`

documentação: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on lift-and-shift and cutover planning.

Sou sre e preciso validar migração Compute on-premises para BioResearch Labs no projeto api-gateway-deployment, em shared-services/uk-london-1, considerando ambiente legado. Qual abordagem você recomenda?

**Disaster Recovery — Onprem Compute (BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicação
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperação
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicação de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Estamos em cenario de auditoria e eu atuo como finops analyst. Como migrar Azure NSGs para Security Lists para cost-optimization em ap-mumbai-1 sem perder de vista equipe enxuta?

**Migration Guide — Azure → OCI (HealthTech Systems - cost-optimization)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (Azure → OCI):**
| Azure | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs Azure

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicações (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em métricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migração OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime

```bash
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
```

**Cenario B: Erro de permissão**

**Performance Tuning — Storage (TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul)**

**Block Volume Performance Tiers:**
| Tier | IOPS/GB | Throughput/GB | Use Case |
|------|---------|---------------|----------|
| Balanced | 10 | 480KB/s | General purpose |
| Higher Performance | 20 | 960KB/s | Databases |
| Ultra High Performance | 75 | 3.6MB/s | High-throughput workloads |

**otimizações:**
- Stripe multiplos Block Volumes para IOPS agregados
- Use NVMe local em Dense I/O shapes para maximo throughput
- Object Storage: multipart upload para arquivos >100MB
- File Storage: ajuste throughput mode (elastic vs provisioned)
- Enable paravirtualized attachment para menor latencia

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Disaster Recovery — Nosql (TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicação
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperação
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicação de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

implementação via OCI CLI para encryption - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**criação do recurso:**
```bash
oci kms key create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "encryption-data-platform-147" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci kms key list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ENABLED'].{name:"display-name",mode:"protection-mode"}"
```

**Atualizar configuração:**
```bash
oci <service> update \
  --key-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci kms key get \
  --key-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci <service> delete \
  --key-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: ``
- OCPUs: `8`
- Storage: `500 GB`

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Monitoring. Provide technical guidance on metrics, alarms, and notifications.
Como configurar Monitoring compliance no OCI para AgriTech Innovations no contexto de hybrid-cloud-integration, com cenario auditoria e requisito de rollback em menos de 15 minutos?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Monitoring (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id devops \
  --display-name "high-cpu-devops" \
  --metric-compartment-id devops \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Disaster Recovery — Onprem Compute (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicação
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperação
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicação de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Compute scaling. Provide technical guidance on instance pools, auto-scaling, and capacity planning.

Estamos em cenario de desativação e eu atuo como devops engineer. Como configurar scaling baseado em memoria para disaster-recovery-setup em eu-frankfurt-1 sem perder de vista com auditoria em 30 dias?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Scaling (FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI security specialist with expertise in Vault keys. Provide technical guidance on key management, encryption, and key policies.
Estamos em cenario de greenfield e eu atuo como cloud architect. Como integrar Vault keys com Block Volume para multi-region-expansion em ap-sydney-1 sem perder de vista mínimo privilégio?

implementação via OCI CLI para vault-keys - MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion:

**criação do recurso:**
```bash
oci kms key create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='analytics'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "vault-keys-analytics-008" \
  --region ap-sydney-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci kms key list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ENABLED'].{name:"display-name",shape:"protection-mode"}"
```

**Atualizar configuração:**
```bash
oci kms key update \
  --key-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "analytics", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci kms key get \
  --key-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci kms key schedule-key-deletion \
  --key-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `analytics`
- Region: `ap-sydney-1`
- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `1`
- Storage: `200 GB`

You are an OCI troubleshooting specialist with expertise in OKE. Provide diagnostic guidance on cluster, node pool, and worker node issues.
Como diagnosticar ingress controller problems no OCI para DataFlow Solutions no contexto de data-lake-modernization, com cenario auditoria e requisito de sem IP público?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Oke (DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id development \
  --display-name "high-cpu-development" \
  --metric-compartment-id development \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in GCP to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on GCP Compute Engine to OCI Compute migration.
Qual desenho você sugere para migrar GCP GPU instances no projeto container-platform da AutoDrive Systems, considerando optimize, equipe enxuta e operação em ap-mumbai-1?

**Migration Guide — GCP → OCI (AutoDrive Systems - container-platform)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (GCP → OCI):**
| GCP | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs GCP

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicações (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em métricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migração OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: migração direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: automação de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in OCI Functions. Provide technical guidance on function deployment, invocation, and monitoring.
No ambiente analytics da MediaStream Pro, qual e a melhor forma de diagnosticar function testing strategies para multi-region-expansion quando o objetivo e review e ha restricao de mínimo privilégio?

**Integration Pattern — Serverless (MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion)**

**Arquitetura:**
```
Internet → API Gateway → OCI Functions → OCI Services
                                    ↓
                    ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
                    ↓               ↓               ↓
              Object Storage   Autonomous DB   OCI Vault
```

**Fluxo de dados:**
1. Client faz request via API Gateway
2. API Gateway autentica e roteia para Function
3. Function processa, acessa servicos OCI via SDK
4. Response retornada ao client

**Exemplo de integração:**
```python
import oci
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data: dict = None):
    object_storage = oci.object_storage.ObjectStorageClient(
        oci.config.from_file()
    )
    namespace = object_storage.get_namespace().data
    object_storage.put_object(
        namespace_name=namespace,
        bucket_name="results",
        object_name="output.json",
        put_object_body=oci.útil.to_stream(data)
    )
    return response.Response(
        ctx, response_data={"status": "success"},
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )
```

**Dynamic Groups para integração:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'analytics'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'analytics'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group analytics-functions to manage objects in compartment analytics
Allow dynamic-group analytics-functions to use secret-family in compartment analytics
```

Guia de diagnostico para secrets - TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul:

**Cenario A: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci vault secret get --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Cenario B: Erro de permissão**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `development`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Cenario C: Timeout de conexão**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Itens para verificação:**
- Estado atual do recurso
- permissões IAM aplicadas
- configuração de networking
- Registros em logs
- métricas de performance

implementação via OCI CLI para instances - TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul:

**criação do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='development'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "instances-development-191" \
  --region us-ashburn-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuração:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "development", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `development`
- Region: `us-ashburn-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `64`
- Storage: `2000 GB`

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Stack Monitoring. Provide technical guidance on resource monitoring and database monitoring.
Como configurar container monitoring OCI no OCI para BioResearch Labs no contexto de api-gateway-deployment, com cenario produção estável e requisito de ambiente legado?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Stack Monitoring (BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id shared-services \
  --display-name "high-cpu-shared-services" \
  --metric-compartment-id shared-services \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

documentação: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/StackMonitoring/overview.htm

**Disaster Recovery — Gcp Compute (BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicação
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperação
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicação de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimização isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: métricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para análise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in API Gateway. Provide technical guidance on routes, integrations, authentication, and throttling.

Sou platform engineer e preciso configurar versioning de APIs para AgriTech Innovations no projeto hybrid-cloud-integration, em devops/me-jeddah-1, considerando rollback em menos de 15 minutos. Qual abordagem você recomenda?

**Integration Pattern — Serverless (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Arquitetura:**
```
Internet → API Gateway → OCI Functions → OCI Services
                                    ↓
                    ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
                    ↓               ↓               ↓
              Object Storage   Autonomous DB   OCI Vault
```

**Fluxo de dados:**
1. Client faz request via API Gateway
2. API Gateway autentica e roteia para Function
3. Function processa, acessa servicos OCI via SDK
4. Response retornada ao client

**Exemplo de integração:**
```python
import oci
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data: dict = None):
    object_storage = oci.object_storage.ObjectStorageClient(
        oci.config.from_file()
    )
    namespace = object_storage.get_namespace().data
    object_storage.put_object(
        namespace_name=namespace,
        bucket_name="results",
        object_name="output.json",
        put_object_body=oci.útil.to_stream(data)
    )
    return response.Response(
        ctx, response_data={"status": "success"},
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )
```

**Dynamic Groups para integração:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to manage objects in compartment devops
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to use secret-family in compartment devops
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

You are an OCI Terraform specialist. Provide technical guidance on Terraform resources for Functions and API Gateway.
Sou dba e preciso gerenciar serverless para RetailMax Online no projeto compliance-audit, em shared-services/uk-london-1, considerando ambiente legado. Qual abordagem você recomenda?

implementação via OCI CLI para serverless - RetailMax Online - compliance-audit:

**criação do recurso:**
```bash
oci fn application create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='shared-services'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "serverless-shared-services-164" \
  --region uk-london-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci fn application list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuração:**
```bash
oci fn application update \
  --application-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "RetailMax Online - compliance-audit"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "shared-services", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci fn application get \
  --application-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci fn application delete \
  --application-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `shared-services`
- Region: `uk-london-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `16`
- Storage: `200 GB`

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in GCP to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on GCP Compute Engine to OCI Compute migration.
Estamos em cenario de desativação e eu atuo como devops engineer. Como migrar GCP confidential VMs para container-platform em ap-mumbai-1 sem perder de vista equipe enxuta?

**Migration Guide — GCP → OCI (AutoDrive Systems - container-platform)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (GCP → OCI):**
| GCP | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs GCP

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicações (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em métricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migração OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: migração direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: automação de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

**Disaster Recovery — Backup Governance (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicação
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperação
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicação de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

- replicação manual via rsync para regiao secundaria
- Mount targets em multiplas ADs para HA

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

```bash
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

- Registros em logs
- métricas de performance

No ambiente analytics da MediaStream Pro, qual e a melhor forma de diagnosticar ingress controller problems para multi-region-expansion quando o objetivo e review e ha restricao de mínimo privilégio?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Oke (MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id analytics \
  --display-name "high-cpu-analytics" \
  --metric-compartment-id analytics \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI audit readiness specialist. Provide technical guidance on audit evidence, logging coverage, traceability, access review, change control, and remediation tracking.
Sou cloud architect e preciso criar audit reports para SmartCity IoT no projeto backup-strategy, em staging/us-phoenix-1, considerando com budget limitado. Qual abordagem você recomenda?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Audit Readiness (SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)



```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`

Qual desenho você sugere para otimizar database costs no projeto database-consolidation da FoodChain Trace, considerando standardize, integração híbrida e operação em ca-toronto-1?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Cost Optimization (FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Disaster Recovery — Database (LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicação
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperação
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicação de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

**Performance Tuning — Storage (AutoDrive Systems - container-platform)**

**Block Volume Performance Tiers:**
| Tier | IOPS/GB | Throughput/GB | Use Case |
|------|---------|---------------|----------|
| Balanced | 10 | 480KB/s | General purpose |
| Higher Performance | 20 | 960KB/s | Databases |
| Ultra High Performance | 75 | 3.6MB/s | High-throughput workloads |

**otimizações:**
- Stripe multiplos Block Volumes para IOPS agregados
- Use NVMe local em Dense I/O shapes para maximo throughput
- Object Storage: multipart upload para arquivos >100MB
- File Storage: ajuste throughput mode (elastic vs provisioned)
- Enable paravirtualized attachment para menor latencia

**Comandos de diagnostico:**

```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

```

**Integration Pattern — Serverless (EduPlatform Global - performance-tuning)**

**Arquitetura:**
```
Internet → API Gateway → OCI Functions → OCI Services
                                    ↓
                    ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
                    ↓               ↓               ↓
              Object Storage   Autonomous DB   OCI Vault
```

**Fluxo de dados:**
1. Client faz request via API Gateway
2. API Gateway autentica e roteia para Function
3. Function processa, acessa servicos OCI via SDK
4. Response retornada ao client

**Exemplo de integração:**
```python
import oci
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data: dict = None):
    object_storage = oci.object_storage.ObjectStorageClient(
        oci.config.from_file()
    )
    namespace = object_storage.get_namespace().data
    object_storage.put_object(
        namespace_name=namespace,
        bucket_name="results",
        object_name="output.json",
        put_object_body=oci.útil.to_stream(data)
    )
    return response.Response(
        ctx, response_data={"status": "success"},
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )
```

**Dynamic Groups para integração:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'security'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'security'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group security-functions to manage objects in compartment security
Allow dynamic-group security-functions to use secret-family in compartment security
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in AWS to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on EC2 to Compute migration and shape mapping.
No ambiente production da TechCorp Brasil, qual e a melhor forma de migrar EC2 Auto Recovery para ecommerce-migration quando o objetivo e design e ha restricao de sem downtime?

**Migration Guide — AWS → OCI (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (AWS → OCI):**
| AWS | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs AWS

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicações (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em métricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migração OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: migração direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: automação de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Compute scaling. Provide technical guidance on instance pools, auto-scaling, and capacity planning.
Estamos em cenario de brownfield e eu atuo como platform engineer. Como configurar pool com shapes flexiveis para data-lake-modernization em us-ashburn-1 sem perder de vista sem IP público?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Scaling (DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id development \
  --display-name "high-cpu-development" \
  --metric-compartment-id development \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in APM. Provide technical guidance on distributed tracing and performance diagnostics.
Qual desenho você sugere para monitorar throughput APM no projeto ecommerce-migration da TechCorp Brasil, considerando design, sem downtime e operação em sa-saopaulo-1?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Apm (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id production \
  --display-name "high-cpu-production" \
  --metric-compartment-id production \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Qual desenho você sugere para diagnosticar cluster networking problems no projeto compliance-audit da RetailMax Online, considerando audit, ambiente legado e operação em uk-london-1?

Guia de diagnostico para oke - RetailMax Online - compliance-audit:

**Cenario A: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci ce cluster get --cluster-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Cenario B: Erro de permissão**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `shared-services`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Cenario C: Timeout de conexão**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Itens para verificação:**
- Estado atual do recurso
- permissões IAM aplicadas
- configuração de networking
- Registros em logs
- métricas de performance

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in AWS to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on S3 to Object Storage migration.
No ambiente production da TechCorp Brasil, qual e a melhor forma de migrar S3 Object Lock para ecommerce-migration quando o objetivo e design e ha restricao de sem downtime?

**Migration Guide — AWS → OCI (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (AWS → OCI):**
| AWS | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs AWS

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicações (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em métricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migração OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: migração direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: automação de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

No ambiente analytics da MediaStream Pro, qual e a melhor forma de resolver scaling problems para multi-region-expansion quando o objetivo e review e ha restricao de mínimo privilégio?

**Performance Tuning — Compute (MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion)**

**métricas criticas:**
| métrica | Threshold | Acao |
|---------|-----------|------|
| CPU Utilization | >80% sustained | Scale up ou adicionar instancias |
| Memory | >85% | Verificar memory leaks, scale up |
| Network throughput | >70% da capacity | Considerar shape com mais bandwidth |
| Disk IOPS | >80% provisionadas | Aumentar performance tier do Block Volume |

**otimizações de shape:**
- VM.Standard.A1.Flex: 1 OCPUs — ideal para workloads balanced
- Flexible shapes: ajuste OCPUs e memory independentemente
- Dense I/O: para workloads com alto IOPS local
- GPU: para ML inference e rendering

**Tuning de sistema operacional:**
- Kernel parameters: net.core.somaxconn, fs.file-max
- TCP: enable TCP BBR congestion control
- Storage: use paravirtualized attachment para latencia menor
- Oracle Cloud Agent: habilitar todos os plugins

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

You are an OCI Terraform specialist. Provide technical guidance on Terraform resources for Logging, Monitoring, and Alarms.
Como gerenciar observability no OCI para RetailMax Online no contexto de compliance-audit, com cenario greenfield e requisito de ambiente legado?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Observability (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id shared-services \
  --display-name "high-cpu-shared-services" \
  --metric-compartment-id shared-services \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure SQL to Autonomous Database migration.
Qual desenho você sugere para migrar Azure SQL managed instance no projeto compliance-audit da RetailMax Online, considerando audit, ambiente legado e operação em uk-london-1?

**Migration Guide — Azure → OCI (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (Azure → OCI):**
| Azure | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs Azure

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicações (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em métricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migração OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: migração direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: automação de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

**Integration Pattern — Compartments (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**Servicos OCI que integram com compartments:**
1. **OCI Vault**: Encryption keys e secrets management
2. **OCI Monitoring**: métricas e alertas
3. **OCI Logging**: Logs estruturados
4. **OCI IAM**: políticas de acesso
5. **OCI Notifications**: Alertas e eventos

**Padrao comum:**
```
Recurso OCI → Events → Service Connector Hub → Destination
                                    ↓
                        ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                        ↓           ↓           ↓
                  Functions   Notifications  Streaming
```

**Dynamic Groups para integração:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'shared-services'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'shared-services'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group shared-services-functions to manage objects in compartment shared-services
Allow dynamic-group shared-services-functions to use secret-family in compartment shared-services
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

You are an OCI troubleshooting specialist with expertise in performance. Provide diagnostic guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and network performance.

Sou devops engineer e preciso diagnosticar CPU utilization problems para AutoDrive Systems no projeto container-platform, em networking/ap-mumbai-1, considerando equipe enxuta. Qual abordagem você recomenda?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Performance (AutoDrive Systems - container-platform)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id networking \
  --display-name "high-cpu-networking" \
  --metric-compartment-id networking \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

implementação via OCI CLI para storage - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**criação do recurso:**
```bash
oci bv volume create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "storage-staging-072" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci bv volume list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='AVAILABLE'].{name:"display-name",size:"size-in-gbs"}"
```

**Atualizar configuração:**
```bash
oci bv volume update \
  --volume-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci bv volume get \
  --volume-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci bv volume delete \
  --volume-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `lex`
- OCPUs: `1`
- Storage: `50 GB`

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in AWS to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on RDS to OCI Database migration.
Qual desenho você sugere para migrar RDS event notifications no projeto performance-tuning da EduPlatform Global, considerando migrate, multi-região e operação em ap-tokyo-1?

**Migration Guide — AWS → OCI (EduPlatform Global - performance-tuning)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (AWS → OCI):**
| AWS | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migração:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs AWS

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**

No ambiente shared-services da BioResearch Labs, qual e a melhor forma de redimensionar Block Volume para api-gateway-deployment quando o objetivo e audit e ha restricao de ambiente legado?

**Performance Tuning — Storage (BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment)**

**Block Volume Performance Tiers:**
| Tier | IOPS/GB | Throughput/GB | Use Case |
|------|---------|---------------|----------|
| Balanced | 10 | 480KB/s | General purpose |
| Higher Performance | 20 | 960KB/s | Databases |
| Ultra High Performance | 75 | 3.6MB/s | High-throughput workloads |

**otimizações:**
- Stripe multiplos Block Volumes para IOPS agregados
- Use NVMe local em Dense I/O shapes para maximo throughput
- Object Storage: multipart upload para arquivos >100MB
- File Storage: ajuste throughput mode (elastic vs provisioned)
- Enable paravirtualized attachment para menor latencia

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

No ambiente networking da HealthTech Systems, qual e a melhor forma de diagnosticar PAR expiration problems para cost-optimization quando o objetivo e optimize e ha restricao de equipe enxuta?

**Disaster Recovery — Storage (HealthTech Systems - cost-optimization)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicação
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperação
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicação de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicação e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**métricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Azure to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Azure VMs to OCI Compute migration.
Sou dba e preciso configurar monitoring Azure-OCI para SmartCity IoT no projeto backup-strategy, em staging/us-phoenix-1, considerando com budget limitado. Qual abordagem você recomenda?

implementação via OCI CLI para azure-compute - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**criação do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "azure-compute-staging-052" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuração:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `BM.GPU4.8`
- OCPUs: `16`
- Storage: `1000 GB`

You are an OCI security specialist with expertise in Vault secrets. Provide technical guidance on secret creation, retrieval, and rotation.
Como automatizar provisioning de secrets no OCI para HealthTech Systems no contexto de cost-optimization, com cenario brownfield e requisito de equipe enxuta?

Guia de diagnostico para vault-secrets - HealthTech Systems - cost-optimization:

**Cenario A: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci vault secret get --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Cenario B: Erro de permissão**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `networking`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Cenario C: Timeout de conexão**

**Performance Tuning — Storage (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Block Volume Performance Tiers:**
| Tier | IOPS/GB | Throughput/GB | Use Case |
|------|---------|---------------|----------|
| Balanced | 10 | 480KB/s | General purpose |
| Higher Performance | 20 | 960KB/s | Databases |
| Ultra High Performance | 75 | 3.6MB/s | High-throughput workloads |

**otimizações:**
- Stripe multiplos Block Volumes para IOPS agregados
- Use NVMe local em Dense I/O shapes para maximo throughput
- Object Storage: multipart upload para arquivos >100MB
- File Storage: ajuste throughput mode (elastic vs provisioned)
- Enable paravirtualized attachment para menor latencia

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

**Integration Pattern — DevOps (SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy)**

**CI/CD Pipeline:**
```
Git Repo → OCI DevOps Build → OCIR → OKE / Compute
                ↓
        OCI DevOps Deploy
                ↓
        OCI Functions / Compute Instance Group
                ↓
        OCI Monitoring (health check)
```

**Infrastructure as Code:**
- Terraform via OCI Resource Manager
- State management em Object Storage
- Plans automaticos em MRs
- Apply via pipeline aprovado

**Dynamic Groups para integração:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'staging'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'staging'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group staging-functions to manage objects in compartment staging
Allow dynamic-group staging-functions to use secret-family in compartment staging
```

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in Logging. Provide technical guidance on log groups, custom logs, and audit logs.
Estamos em cenario de desativação e eu atuo como devops engineer. Como configurar retention policies logs para security-hardening em ca-toronto-1 sem perder de vista integração híbrida?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Logging (LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening)**

**métricas essenciais:**
| métrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**configuração de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (análise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

- Registros em logs
- métricas de performance
